item.title: Drua Women crush Reds 50-17 in dominant display
item.description: The McDonald's Fiji Drua Women's team have secured a home semi-final after producing a dominant display to defeat the Reds 50-17, running in a series of impressive tries while showcasing their attacking flair and rock-solid defence. The Drua were in complete control during the opening half.
item.content:encoded:
The McDonald's Fiji Drua Women's team have secured a home semi-final after producing a dominant display to defeat the Reds 50-17, running in a series of impressive tries while showcasing their attacking flair and rock-solid defence.

The Drua were in complete control during the opening half.

In the 28th minute, Drua crossed over for another try to give them a commanding 21-0 lead.

Qoro sliced through the Reds' defence before delivering a perfectly timed pass to Aqela Biutu, who finished the move to extend the advantage to 26-0.

The Reds finally found a breakthrough in the 38th minute with their first try of the match, reducing the deficit to 26-5.

However, the Drua responded immediately.

Applying relentless pressure, they forced another opening and crossed over in the 39th minute to head into halftime with a comfortable 33-5 lead.

The second half began with the Reds looking for a way back into the contest.

Eva Kapani, the Reds Prop, produced a strong line break shortly after the restart, but the Drua's defence held firm to deny the home side.

Both teams created attacking opportunities during the opening stages of the half, but handling errors and strong defensive efforts prevented either side from adding to the scoreboard.

The breakthrough eventually came in the 49th minute when Litiana Vueti scored another well-worked try, stretching the Drua's lead to 40-5.

The Drua continued to apply pressure in attack, although they lost possession after building momentum in the 60th minute.

Four minutes later, the Reds mounted several attacking phases deep inside the Drua's territory, Drua side's determined defence stood tall to keep them scoreless.

The Drua then launched one of the most impressive attacks of the match in the 67th minute.

After patiently building through 11 phases, they finally broke the Reds' defence to score another try and extend their lead to 47-5.

After more than 20 phases of relentless pressure in the 73rd minute, the Reds earned a penalty and eventually crossed for a try two minutes later to cut the score to 47-10.

They added another late try in the 79th minute to narrow the margin further to 47-17.

With the final play of the match, Qoro calmly slotted a penalty goal to bring up the half-century, sealing an emphatic 50-17 victory for the Drua Women.

The convincing win highlighted the Drua's clinical finishing, disciplined defence, and ability to dominate possession, as they outclassed the Reds from start to finish.

28 th minute McDonald's women scores another try to Robanakadavu to lead 21-0

29 minute Qoro splits the defense open, delivers a pass to Aqela Biutu to score another try to extend their lead 26-0.

38 th minute Reds score their first try in 38 th minute Drua lead 26-5.

39 th minute Drua scores another try to extend their lead after pressuring the Reds 33-5.

40th minute, Reds kicks off and Eva Kapani breaks the Reds line but Drua defense too strong for her.

46th minute both team attacking but not finishing as expected.

49th minute Litiana Vueti scores another try to extend their lead 40-5.

60th minute Drua attacking Reds line showing momentum and losses possesion.

64th minute reds launch multiple attack phases on Drua line and fail to score any points after another strong defense.

67th minute Drua causing havoc attacking the Reds line into the 11 phase and scores a try to extend their lead 47-5.

73th After 20 phases, the Reds attacked the Drua line they could not score but get another chance through a penalty.

item.title: Wales beat Barbarians 33-31 as North signs off in style
item.description: Wales beat Barbarians 33-31 as North signs off in style

item.title: Navua to face Ba in Fiji FACT final
item.description: Navua will take on Ba in the final of the BiC Fiji FACT final after defeating Lautoka 2-1 in an exciting semi-final clash as Subrail Park. Both teams played entertaining football in the first half, creating several chances, but neither side was able to find the back of the net as the match remained scoreless at halftime.
item.content:encoded:
Navua will take on Ba in the final of the BiC Fiji FACT final after defeating Lautoka 2-1 in an exciting semi-final clash as Subrail Park.

Both teams played entertaining football in the first half, creating several chances, but neither side was able to find the back of the net as the match remained scoreless at halftime.

Lautoka came out strongly after the break and earned a penalty early in the second half.

Striker Rusiate Matererega calmly converted from the spot to give the Blues a 1-0 lead.

Navua responded positively, increasing the pressure on Lautoka's defence.

Their persistence paid off when Thomas Dunn scored the equaliser to make it 1-1.

The match remained deadlocked at full-time, forcing extra time.

In the second spell of extra time, Thomas Dunn struck again with the winning goal and send Navua into the final with a 2-1 victory.

Lautoka continued to press in search of another equaliser, but Navua's defence stood firm to deny every opportunity.

Navua coach Roderic Singh praised his players for their determination.

Sigh says the boys were outstanding and even after going a goal down, they never lost hope.

He says they kept believing, kept applying the pressure, and deserved the win.

Singh has dedicated the victory to his wife.

Ba beat Labasa 1-0 in the first semi-final.

Navua will now face Ba at 3 this afternoon.

item.title: Baulevu High School seal 1-0 win over Bhawani Dayal Arya
item.description: Baulevu High School has kicked off its Rewa Secondary Schools Football Association Under-17 Pool A campaign with a 1-0 win over Bhawani Dayal Arya College at Saraswati Manoca Ground. The lone goal of the match came in the 10th minute when Inia Robert T.R. found the back of the net to hand Baulevu all three points.
item.content:encoded:
Baulevu High School has kicked off its Rewa Secondary Schools Football Association Under-17 Pool A campaign with a 1-0 win over Bhawani Dayal Arya College at Saraswati Manoca Ground.

The lone goal of the match came in the 10th minute when Inia Robert T.R. found the back of the net to hand Baulevu all three points.

Despite Bhawani Dayal Arya College creating several opportunities, Baulevu's defence stood firm to secure the victory.

Meanwhile, Lomaivuna High School and Saraswati College played out a hard-fought 0-0 draw.

Source: Rewa Secondary School Football Association

item.title: Ba beat Labasa 1-0 to book place in Fiji FACT final
item.description: Mohammed Feroz , Nilufa Buksh · 27/06/2026 Ba has booked its place in the BiC Fiji FACT final after defeating Labasa 1-0 in a thrilling semi-final at Subrail Park.
item.content:encoded:
Mohammed Feroz , Nilufa Buksh · 27/06/2026

Ba has booked its place in the BiC Fiji FACT final after defeating Labasa 1-0 in a thrilling semi-final at Subrail Park.

Both teams produced an entertaining display of football, with Ba dominating possession in the opening half and creating several scoring opportunities through their impressive passing game.

However, they were unable to capitalise on their chances.

Labasa also looked dangerous on the counterattack and created opportunities of their own, but neither side managed to break the deadlock as the match remained scoreless at half-time.

The second half continued at a fast pace, with both teams pushing hard for the opening goal.

Despite several attacking moves from both sides, neither team was able to find the back of the net, forcing the match into extra time after a goalless 90 minutes.

Ba finally made the breakthrough in the first spell of extra time when Etonia Dogalau scored what proved to be the winning goal to put the Men in Black ahead 1-0.

Labasa's hopes of staging a comeback suffered a major setback in the second spell of extra time after Iliesa Nayasi was shown a red card, reducing the Babasiga Lions to 10 men.

Ba defended resolutely for the remainder of the match to seal the victory and secure a place in tomorrow's BIC Fiji FACT final.

Following the win, Ba Football Association President Praneel Dayal says he was extremely proud of the team's performance.

Dayal praised the players for their outstanding display and expressed confidence that the BIC Fiji FACT title would return to Ba.

He also calls on Ba supporters to turn out in large numbers for the final and thanked the loyal fans who packed Subrail Park , creating a vibrant atmosphere with their musical instruments and unwavering support throughout the match.

Ba will now await the winner of the second semi-final as they look to lift the BIC Fiji FACT trophy.

Meanwhile, the final will be played tomorrow at 3pm.

item.title: Spain edge Uruguay 1-0 to finish Group H in FIFA World Cup
item.description: Spain secured a hard-fought 1-0 victory over Uruguay in their final FIFA World Cup Group H match. Spain led 1-0 at half-time and held on to claim all three points, while Uruguay finished the match with 10 men after a late red card in stoppage time.
item.content:encoded:
Spain secured a hard-fought 1-0 victory over Uruguay in their final FIFA World Cup Group H match.

Spain led 1-0 at half-time and held on to claim all three points, while Uruguay finished the match with 10 men after a late red card in stoppage time.

In other matches, Cape Verde and Saudi Arabia finished 0-0, France beat Norway 4-1, and Senegal thrashed Iraq 5-0.

Egypt face Iran and New Zealand take on Belgium at 3.00pm.

item.title: Navua FC determined to end long BiC Fiji FACT Title drought
item.description: Mohammed Feroz , Nilufa Buksh · 27/06/2026 Navua FC is aiming to create history by bringing the prestigious BiC Fiji FACT trophy back home after many years.
item.content:encoded:
Mohammed Feroz , Nilufa Buksh · 27/06/2026

Navua FC is aiming to create history by bringing the prestigious BiC Fiji FACT trophy back home after many years.

Speaking ahead of today's second semi-final, Navua head coach Roderic Singh says the club has not won the Fiji FACT title for many years, and the players are determined to end that drought.

Singh believes the semi-final against Lautoka will be a tough contest but says his side are ready for the challenge.

He says the players worked extremely hard during the pool matches to secure their place in the semi-finals and are grateful for the opportunity to compete for a place in the final.

The Navua coach adds that the squad are excited and highly motivated ahead of the crucial clash .

Insert: Singh on missed opporunities 27th June.

Singh is also calling on Navua supporters to keep the team in their prayers and continue backing them throughout the tournament.

Meanwhile, the first semi-final kicks off at 2.30pm, with Labasa taking on Ba and Navua taking Lautoka at 4.30pm.

item.title: Labasa and Savusavu legends to clash before Fiji FACT final
item.description: Former football stars from Labasa and Savusavu will reignite one of the North's traditional rivalries when they meet in a Legends
